}\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\timpl From<crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig> for SessionPinningConfig {\n65\t    fn from(config: crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n66\t struct SessionState {\n79\t    /// Last write miroir task ID (if any).\n80\t    pub last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n81\t    /// Last write timestamp.\n82\t    pub last_write_at: u64,\n83\t    /// Pinned replica group ID.\n84\t    pub pinned_group: Option<u32>,\n85\t    /// Minimum settings version observed by this session.\n86\t    pub min_settings_version: u64,\n87\t    /// Session created at.\n88\t    pub created_at: u64,\n89\t    /// Session expires at.\n90\t    pub expires_at: u64,\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\timpl SessionState {\n94\t    /// Check if this session is expired.\n95\t    pub fn is_expired(&self) -> bool {\n96\t        let now = millis_now();\n97\t        now > self.expires_at\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Check if there's a pending write.\n101\t    pub fn has_pending_write(&self) -> bool {\n102\t        self.last_write_mtask_id.is_some()\n103\t    }\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\t/// Session pinning manager.\n107\tpub struct SessionManager {\n108\t    /// Configuration.\n109\t    config: SessionPinningConfig,\n110\t    /// Session ID -> Session state (IndexMap maintains insertion order for LRU).\n111\t    sessions: Arc<RwLock<IndexMap<String, SessionState>>>,\n112\t    /// Per-index pending writes (session_id -> mtask_id).\n113\t    pending_writes: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>>>,\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl SessionManager {\n117\t    /// Create a new session manager.\n118\t    pub fn new(config: SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n119\t        Self {\n120\t            config,\n121\t            sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(IndexMap::new())),\n122\t            pending_writes: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n123\t        }\n124\t    }\n125\t\n126\t    /// Record a write for a session.\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// Returns the group ID that was pinned (first to reach quorum).\n129\t    ///\n130\t    /// This method should be called AFTER per-group quorum is achieved,\n131\t    /// with the `first_quorum_group` being the first group to reach quorum.\n132\t    pub async fn record_write_with_quorum(\n133\t        &self,\n134\t        session_id: &str,\n135\t        mtask_id: String,\n136\t        first_quorum_group: u32,\n137\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n138\t        if !self.config.enabled {\n139\t            return Ok(());\n140\t        }\n141\t\n142\t        let now = millis_now();\n143\t        let expires_at = now + (self.config.ttl_seconds * 1000);\n144\t\n145\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n146\t\n147\t        // Enforce max sessions (simple FIFO - remove oldest entry when at capacity)\n148\t        if sessions.len() >= self.config.max_sessions as usize {\n149\t            // Remove oldest entry (first key in HashMap)\n150\t            if let Some(key) = sessions.keys().next().cloned() {\n151\t                sessions.remove(&key);\n152\t                debug!(session_id = %key, \"evicted oldest session to enforce max_sessions\");\n153\t            }\n154\t        }\n155\t\n156\t        // Get or create session\n157\t        let session = sessions.entry(session_id.to_string()).or_insert(SessionState {\n158\t            last_write_mtask_id: None,\n159\t            last_write_at: 0,\n160\t            pinned_group: None,\n161\t            min_settings_version: 0,\n162\t            created_at: now,\n163\t            expires_at,\n164\t        });\n165\t\n166\t        // Update session state\n167\t        session.last_write_mtask_id = Some(mtask_id.clone());\n168\t        session.last_write_at = now;\n169\t        session.expires_at = expires_at;\n170\t\n171\t        // Pin the group if not already pinned (first write wins)\n172\t        if session.pinned_group.is_none() {\n173\t            session.pinned_group = Some(first_quorum_group);\n174\t            info!(\n175\t                session_id = %session_id,\n176\t                mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n177\t                pinned_group = first_quorum_group,\n178\t                \"session pinned to first-quorum group\"\n179\t            );\n180\t        } else {\n181\t            debug!(\n182\t                session_id = %session_id,\n183\t                existing_group = session.pinned_group,\n184\t                new_quorum_group = first_quorum_group,\n185\t                \"session already pinned, ignoring new quorum group\"\n186\t            );\n187\t        }\n188\t\n189\t        // Track pending write per session\n190\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n191\t        pending.entry(session_id.to_string())\n192\t            .or_insert_with(HashMap::new)\n193\t            .insert(mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group.to_string());\n194\t\n195\t        Ok(())\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    /// Wait for a pending write to complete (block strategy).\n199\t    ///\n200\t    /// Polls the task registry until the write succeeds or times out.\n201\t    pub async fn wait_for_write_completion<T: TaskRegistry + ?Sized>(\n202\t        &self,\n203\t        session_id: &str,\n204\t        task_registry: &Arc<T>,\n205\t        max_wait: Duration,\n206\t    ) -> Result<bool> {\n207\t        let session = {\n208\t            let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n209\t            sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n210\t        };\n211\t\n212\t        let session = session.ok_or_else(|| {\n213\t            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"session not found\".to_string())\n214\t        })?;\n215\t\n216\t        let mtask_id = session.last_write_mtask_id.ok_or_else(|| {\n217\t            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"no pending write for session\".to_string())\n218\t        })?;\n219\t\n220\t        let start = SystemTime::now();\n221\t        let mut poll_delay = 25; // Start with 25ms\n222\t\n223\t        loop {\n224\t            // Check task status\n225\t            if let Ok(Some(task)) = task_registry.get(&mtask_id) {\n226\t                match task.status {\n227\t                    TaskStatus::Succeeded => {\n228\t                        // Clear pending write state\n229\t                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n230\t                        return Ok(true);\n231\t                    }\n232\t                    TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled => {\n233\t                        // Clear pending write state even on failure\n234\t                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n235\t                        return Ok(false);\n236\t                    }\n237\t                    _ => {}\n238\t                }\n239\t            }\n240\t\n241\t            // Check timeout\n242\t            let elapsed = start.elapsed().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n243\t            if elapsed >= max_wait {\n244\t                warn!(\n245\t                    session_id = %session_id,\n246\t                    mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n247\t                    elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),\n248\t                    max_wait_ms = max_wait.as_millis(),\n249\t                    \"session pin wait timeout\"\n250\t                );\n251\t                return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"session pin wait timeout\".to_string()));\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n255\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_delay)).await;\n256\t            poll_delay = std::cmp::min(poll_delay * 2, 500); // Cap at 500ms\n257\t        }\n258\t    }\n259\t\n260\t    /// Get the pinned group for a session (if any).\n261\t    ///\n262\t    /// Returns None if:\n263\t    /// - Session doesn't exist\n264\t    /// - Session has no pending write\n265\t    /// - Session is expired\n266\t    pub async fn get_pinned_group(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<u32> {\n267\t        if !self.config.enabled {\n268\t            return None;\n269\t        }\n270\t\n271\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n272\t        let session = sessions.get(session_id)?;\n273\t\n274\t        if session.is_expired() {\n275\t            return None;\n276\t        }\n277\t\n278\t        if !session.has_pending_write() {\n279\t            return None;\n280\t        }\n281\t\n282\t        session.pinned_group\n283\t    }\n284\t\n285\t    /// Clear the pending write state for a session.\n286\t    ///\n287\t    /// Called when the write task completes.\n288\t    pub async fn clear_pending_write(&self, session_id: &str) {\n289\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n290\t        pending.remove(session_id);\n291\t\n292\t        // Also clear the last_write_mtask_id in the session\n293\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n294\t        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n295\t            session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    /// Get session state.\n300\t    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionState> {\n301\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n302\t        sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /// Delete a session.\n306\t    pub async fn delete_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> bool {\n307\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n308\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n309\t        pending.remove(session_id);\n310\t        sessions.remove(session_id).is_some()\n311\t    }\n312\t\n313\t    /// Clean up expired sessions.\n314\t    pub async fn prune_expired(&self) -> usize {\n315\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n316\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n317\t\n318\t        let now = millis_now();\n319\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n320\t\n321\t        for (id, session) in sessions.iter() {\n322\t            if session.is_expired() {\n323\t                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n324\t            }\n325\t        }\n326\t\n327\t        for id in &to_remove {\n328\t            sessions.remove(id);\n329\t            pending.remove(id);\n330\t        }\n331\t\n332\t        to_remove.len()\n333\t    }\n334\t\n335\t    /// Get current session count.\n336\t    pub async fn session_count(&self) -> usize {\n337\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n338\t        sessions.len()\n339\t    }\n340\t\n341\t    /// Handle pinned group failure - clear the pin for a session.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Called when the pinned group for a session becomes unavailable.\n344\t    /// Subsequent reads will use normal routing.\n345\t    pub async fn handle_pinned_group_failure(&self, session_id: &str, failed_group: u32) -> bool {\n346\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n347\t        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n348\t            if session.pinned_group == Some(failed_group) {\n349\t                info!(\n350\t                    session_id = %session_id,\n351\t                    failed_group,\n352\t                    \"clearing session pin due to group failure\"\n353\t                );\n354\t                session.pinned_group = None;\n355\t                // Also clear pending write state since we can't guarantee visibility\n356\t                session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n357\t                return true;\n358\t            }\n359\t        }\n360\t        false\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    /// Get the wait strategy.\n364\t    pub fn wait_strategy(&self) -> WaitStrategy {\n365\t        match self.config.wait_strategy.as_str() {\n366\t            \"block\" => WaitStrategy::Block,\n367\t            \"route_pin\" => WaitStrategy::RoutePin,\n368\t            _ => WaitStrategy::Block,\n369\t        }\n370\t    }\n371\t\n372\t    /// Get max wait metric.\n378\t    pub fn update_metrics(&self, active_count_fn: impl FnOnce(usize)) {\n379\t        let count = self.sessions.blocking_read().len();\n380\t        active_count_fn(count);\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    /// Check if session pinning is enabled.\n384\t    pub fn is_enabled(&self) -> bool {\n385\t        self.config.enabled\n386\t    }\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t/// Wait strategy for reads with pending writes.\n390\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n391\tpub enum WaitStrategy {\n392\t    /// Block the read until the write completes.\n393\t    Block,\n394\t    /// Route to pinned group but don't wait for write.\n395\t    RoutePin,\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\timpl Default for SessionManager {\n399\t    fn default() -> Self {\n400\t        Self::new(SessionPinningConfig::default())\n401\t    }\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n405\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n406\t    SystemTime::now()\n407\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n408\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n409\t        .as_millis() as u64\n410\t}\n411\t\n412\t#[cfg(test)]\n413\tmod tests {\n414\t    use super::*;\n415\t\n416\t    #[test]\n417\t    fn test_config_default() {\n418\t        let config = SessionPinningConfig::default();\n419\t        assert!(config.enabled);\n420\t        assert_eq!(config.ttl_seconds, 900);\n421\t        assert_eq!(config.max_sessions, 100_000);\n422\t        assert_eq!(config.wait_strategy, \"block\");\n423\t        assert_eq!(config.max_wait_ms, 5000);\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    #[tokio::test]\n427\t    async fn test_record_write() {\n428\t        let manager = SessionManager::default();\n429\t        manager\n430\t            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-123\".into(), 0)\n431\t            .await\n432\t            .unwrap();\n433\t\n434\t        let session = manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.unwrap();\n435\t Session pinning for read-your-writes consistency (plan §13.6).\n//!\n//! Clients provide X-Miroir-Session header; Miroir tracks pending writes\n//! and routes subsequent reads to the pinned replica group.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task::{TaskRegistry, TaskStatus};\nuse indexmap::IndexMap;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// Session pinning configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SessionPinningConfig {\n    /// Whether session pinning is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Session TTL in seconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_ttl\")]\n    pub ttl_seconds: u64,\n    /// Maximum number of sessions.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_sessions\")]\n    pub max_sessions: u32,\n    /// Wait strategy: \"block\" or \"route_pin\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_wait_strategy\")]\n    pub wait_strategy: String,\n    /// Maximum wait time in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_wait\")]\n    pub max_wait_ms: u64,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_ttl() -> u64 {\n    900\n}\nfn default_max_sessions() -> u32 {\n    100_000\n}\nfn default_wait_strategy() -> String {\n    \"block\".into()\n}\nfn default_max_wait() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionPinningConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            ttl_seconds: default_ttl(),\n            max_sessions: default_max_sessions(),\n            wait_strategy: default_wait_strategy(),\n            max_wait_ms: default_max_wait(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig> for SessionPinningConfig {\n    fn from(config: crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: config.enabled,\n            ttl_seconds: config.ttl_seconds,\n            max_sessions: config.max_sessions,\n            wait_strategy: config.wait_strategy,\n            max_wait_ms: config.max_wait_ms,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SessionState {\n    /// Last write miroir task ID (if any).\n    pub last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Last write timestamp.\n    pub last_write_at: u64,\n    /// Pinned replica group ID.\n    pub pinned_group: Option<u32>,\n    /// Minimum settings version observed by this session.\n    pub min_settings_version: u64,\n    /// Session created at.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Session expires at.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\nimpl SessionState {\n    /// Check if this session is expired.\n    pub fn is_expired(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        now > self.expires_at\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there's a pending write.\n    pub fn has_pending_write(&self) -> bool {\n        self.last_write_mtask_id.is_some()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session pinning manager.\npub struct SessionManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: SessionPinningConfig,\n    /// Session ID -> Session state (IndexMap maintains insertion order for LRU).\n    sessions: Arc<RwLock<IndexMap<String, SessionState>>>,\n    /// Per-index pending writes (session_id -> mtask_id).\n    pending_writes: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>>>,\n}\n\nimpl SessionManager {\n    /// Create a new session manager.\n    pub fn new(config: SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(IndexMap::new())),\n            pending_writes: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a write for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the group ID that was pinned (first to reach quorum).\n    ///\n    /// This method should be called AFTER per-group quorum is achieved,\n    /// with the `first_quorum_group` being the first group to reach quorum.\n    pub async fn record_write_with_quorum(\n        &self,\n        session_id: &str,\n        mtask_id: String,\n        first_quorum_group: u32,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let expires_at = now + (self.config.ttl_seconds * 1000);\n\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n\n        // Enforce max sessions (simple FIFO - remove oldest entry when at capacity)\n        if sessions.len() >= self.config.max_sessions as usize {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key in HashMap)\n            if let Some(key) = sessions.keys().next().cloned() {\n                sessions.remove(&key);\n                debug!(session_id = %key, \"evicted oldest session to enforce max_sessions\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Get or create session\n        let session = sessions.entry(session_id.to_string()).or_insert(SessionState {\n            last_write_mtask_id: None,\n            last_write_at: 0,\n            pinned_group: None,\n            min_settings_version: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n\n        // Update session state\n        session.last_write_mtask_id = Some(mtask_id.clone());\n        session.last_write_at = now;\n        session.expires_at = expires_at;\n\n        // Pin the group if not already pinned (first write wins)\n        if session.pinned_group.is_none() {\n            session.pinned_group = Some(first_quorum_group);\n            info!(\n                session_id = %session_id,\n                mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n                pinned_group = first_quorum_group,\n                \"session pinned to first-quorum group\"\n            );\n        } else {\n            debug!(\n                session_id = %session_id,\n                existing_group = session.pinned_group,\n                new_quorum_group = first_quorum_group,\n                \"session already pinned, ignoring new quorum group\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Track pending write per session\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.entry(session_id.to_string())\n            .or_insert_with(HashMap::new)\n            .insert(mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group.to_string());\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Wait for a pending write to complete (block strategy).\n    ///\n    /// Polls the task registry until the write succeeds or times out.\n    pub async fn wait_for_write_completion<T: TaskRegistry + ?Sized>(\n        &self,\n        session_id: &str,\n        task_registry: &Arc<T>,\n        max_wait: Duration,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let session = {\n            let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n            sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n        };\n\n        let session = session.ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"session not found\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let mtask_id = session.last_write_mtask_id.ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"no pending write for session\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let start = SystemTime::now();\n        let mut poll_delay = 25; // Start with 25ms\n\n        loop {\n            // Check task status\n            if let Ok(Some(task)) = task_registry.get(&mtask_id) {\n                match task.status {\n                    TaskStatus::Succeeded => {\n                        // Clear pending write state\n                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled => {\n                        // Clear pending write state even on failure\n                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n                        return Ok(false);\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Check timeout\n            let elapsed = start.elapsed().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n            if elapsed >= max_wait {\n                warn!(\n                    session_id = %session_id,\n                    mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n                    elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),\n                    max_wait_ms = max_wait.as_millis(),\n                    \"session pin wait timeout\"\n                );\n                return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"session pin wait timeout\".to_string()));\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_delay)).await;\n            poll_delay = std::cmp::min(poll_delay * 2, 500); // Cap at 500ms\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the pinned group for a session (if any).\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if:\n    /// - Session doesn't exist\n    /// - Session has no pending write\n    /// - Session is expired\n    pub async fn get_pinned_group(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<u32> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        let session = sessions.get(session_id)?;\n\n        if session.is_expired() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        if !session.has_pending_write() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        session.pinned_group\n    }\n\n    /// Clear the pending write state for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Called when the write task completes.\n    pub async fn clear_pending_write(&self, session_id: &str) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.remove(session_id);\n\n        // Also clear the last_write_mtask_id in the session\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get session state.\n    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionState> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Delete a session.\n    pub async fn delete_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> bool {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.remove(session_id);\n        sessions.remove(session_id).is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up expired sessions.\n    pub async fn prune_expired(&self) -> usize {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n\n        for (id, session) in sessions.iter() {\n            if session.is_expired() {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        for id in &to_remove {\n            sessions.remove(id);\n            pending.remove(id);\n        }\n\n        to_remove.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get current session count.\n    pub async fn session_count(&self) -> usize {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Handle pinned group failure - clear the pin for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Called when the pinned group for a session becomes unavailable.\n    /// Subsequent reads will use normal routing.\n    pub async fn handle_pinned_group_failure(&self, session_id: &str, failed_group: u32) -> bool {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            if session.pinned_group == Some(failed_group) {\n                info!(\n                    session_id = %session_id,\n                    failed_group,\n                    \"clearing session pin due to group failure\"\n                );\n                session.pinned_group = None;\n                // Also clear pending write state since we can't guarantee visibility\n                session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n                return true;\n            }\n        }\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Get the wait strategy.\n    pub fn wait_strategy(&self) -> WaitStrategy {\n        match self.config.wait_strategy.as_str() {\n            \"block\" => WaitStrategy::Block,\n            \"route_pin\" => WaitStrategy::RoutePin,\n            _ => WaitStrategy::Block,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get max wait duration.\n    pub fn max_wait_duration(&self) -> Duration {\n        Duration::from_millis(self.config.max_wait_ms)\n    }\n\n    /// Update the session active count metric.\n    pub fn update_metrics(&self, active_count_fn: impl FnOnce(usize)) {\n        let count = self.sessions.blocking_read().len();\n        active_count_fn(count);\n    }\n\n    /// Check if session pinning is enabled.\n    pub fn is_enabled(&self) -> bool {\n        self.config.enabled\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wait strategy for reads with pending writes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WaitStrategy {\n    /// Block the read until the write completes.\n    Block,\n    /// Route to pinned group but don't wait for write.\n    RoutePin,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(SessionPinningConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = SessionPinningConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.ttl_seconds, 900);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_sessions, 100_000);\n        assert_eq!(config.wait_strategy, \"block\");\n        assert_eq!(config.max_wait_ms, 5000);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_write() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::default();\n        manager\n            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-123\".into(), 0)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let session = manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(session.last_write_mtask_id, Some(\"mtask-123\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(session.pinned_group, Some(0));\n        assert!(session.has_pending_write());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_pinned_group() {\n        let manager = See §13.5.\n978-- `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` — client write targeted a multi-target alias managed by §13.17 ILM (HTTP 409). See §13.7.\n979-- `miroir_jwt_invalid` — bearer token parsed as a JWT but failed validation (bad signature, expired, wrong `kid`/`alg`, or malformed claims) (HTTP 401). See §13.21 and \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n980-- `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` — JWT signature, expiry, and index-binding are valid, but the token's `scope` array does not include the action for this method+path combination, or the `idx` claim does not match the target index (HTTP 403). Enforced orchestrator-side before any node call; see §13.21.\n981-- `miroir_invalid_auth` — credentials did not match any expected key (master, admin, or valid search UI JWT) on an auth-gated endpoint (HTTP 401). See \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n982-\n983-### Reserved fields\n984-\n985-[Omitted long context line]\n986-\n987-| Field | Type | Reserved when | Purpose |\n988-|-------|------|---------------|---------|\n989-| `_miroir_shard` | integer | Always (unconditional) | Shard identity injected at write; stored as a filterable attribute; used by the rebalancer (Section 4) and anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) for targeted shard-level retrieval. |\n990-| `_miroir_updated_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8) | Logical write timestamp used as the authoritative-version tie-breaker during anti-entropy repair. |\n991-| `_miroir_expires_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `ttl.enabled: true` (§13.14) | Per-document expiration timestamp consumed by the TTL sweeper. Added to `filterableAttributes` automatically at index creation via the two-phase settings broadcast when TTL is enabled. |\n992-\n993-When anti-entropy is disabled, `_miroir_updated_at` is not reserved: client values in a field of that name are preserved and passed through untouched. When anti-entropy is enabled, clients must not submit `_miroir_updated_at`; the orchestrator stamps it on every write and rejects client-supplied values with `miroir_reserved_field`.\n994-\n995-When TTL is disabled, `_miroir_expires_at` is not reserved and client values pass through untouched. When TTL is enabled, clients set `_miroir_expires_at` on documents that should expire; the orchestrator does not stamp it, but rejects reads that attempt to project it (it is always stripped from responses) and the sweeper (§13.14) deletes documents whose `_miroir_expires_at <= now_ms`.\n996-\n997-### Custom HTTP headers\n998-\n999-Miroir adds a small set of custom headers on top of the Meilisearch REST API. All Miroir-specific headers use the `X-Miroir-` prefix; the one exception is `Idempotency-Key`, which follows the widely recognized cross-vendor convention. Clients that do not recognize these headers MUST ignore them — Meilisearch-compatibility is preserved. Unless otherwise noted, the headers are safe to strip or forward through intermediaries.\n1000-\n1001-| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n1002-|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n1003-| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n1004-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n1005-| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n1006-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n1007:| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n1008-| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n1009-| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n1010-| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n1011-| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n1012-| `X-CSRF-Token` | Request | Admin UI CSRF double-submit token; required on state-changing requests from the Admin UI session cookie path. Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n1013-| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n1014-\n1015-### Bearer token dispatch\n1016-\n1017-Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n1018-\n1019-[Omitted long context line]\n1020-\n1021-[Omitted long context line]\n1022-2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n1023-   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n1024-   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n1025-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n1026-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n1027-3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n1028-4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n1029-[Omitted long context line]\n1030-   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n1031-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n1032-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n1033-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode` (`public` / `shared_key` via `X-Search-UI-Key` header / `oauth_proxy` via upstream-proxy headers); see §13.21.\n1034-   - `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point (static assets); IP rate-limited only.\n1035-\n1036-`X-Admin-Key` (admin endpoints) and `X-Search-UI-Key` (session-minting endpoint) are evaluated independently of the bearer chain above — they short-circuit directly into their respective code paths.\n1037-\n--\n2383-\n2384-**Problem.** Section 3 flags settings broadcast as \"the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle.\" The current sequential apply-with-rollback leaves a non-atomic window where some nodes have new settings and others have old, producing non-comparable `_rankingScore` values and corrupting merged search results (Open Problem 4).\n2385-\n2386-**Mechanism.** Replace the sequential flow with propose / verify / commit. Meilisearch has no dry-run, so verification is read-back:\n2387-\n2388-```\n2389-Phase 1 — Propose (parallel):\n2390-    for each node:\n2391-        PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings   (new settings)\n2392-        task_uid = await\n2393-    wait for all task_uids to reach \"succeeded\"\n2394-\n2395-Phase 2 — Verify (parallel):\n2396-    for each node:\n2397-        actual      = GET /indexes/{uid}/settings\n2398-        actual_hash = sha256(canonical_json(actual))\n2399-    all hashes must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed))\n2400-\n2401-Phase 3 — Commit:\n2402-    if verify ok: increment settings_version in task store;\n2403-                  stamp X-Miroir-Settings-Version on future responses\n2404-    if diverge:   reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair);\n2405-                  after max_retries, freeze writes on that index and\n2406-                  raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.\n2407-```\n2408-\n2409-During phases 1–2 the orchestrator returns 202 with a \"pending\" status; clients poll `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}`. Reads during that window include an `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.\n2410-\n2411-**Drift reconciler (always on).** A background task runs every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5m), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. This catches out-of-band changes (direct access to a node) and cures drift without operator intervention.\n2412-\n2413:[Omitted long matching line]\n2414-\n2415-**Compatibility.** Uses `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings` and `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` — both public endpoints.\n2416-\n2417-**Config.**\n2418-```yaml\n2419-miroir:\n2420-  settings_broadcast:\n2421-    strategy: two_phase             # two_phase | sequential (legacy)\n2422-    verify_timeout_s: 60\n2423-    max_repair_retries: 3\n2424-    freeze_writes_on_unrepairable: true\n2425-  settings_drift_check:\n2426-    interval_s: 300\n2427-    auto_repair: true\n2428-```\n2429-\n2430-**Metrics.** `miroir_settings_broadcast_phase` (gauge), `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total`, `miroir_settings_drift_repair_total`, `miroir_settings_version` (gauge; increments only on successful commit).\n2431-\n2432----\n2433-\n2434-### 13.6 Read-your-writes via session pinning\n2435-\n2436-**Problem.** Miroir is eventually consistent per the task reconciliation model. Clients reading immediately after writing race against node task processing and frequently fail. SDKs work around this by polling task status — clumsy and error-prone.\n2437-\n2438-**Mechanism.** Introduce a session identified by `X-Miroir-Session: <opaque-uuid>` (clients generate and persist the value). Session state in the task store:\n2439-\n2440-```\n2441-session_id → {\n2442-    last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n2443-    last_write_at: Instant,\n--\n2688-**Mechanism.** `POST /multi-search` with `{\"queries\": [{indexUid, q, filter, ...}, ...]}`. Each query in the batch is scattered independently and in parallel; results are returned in input order with individual status codes. Each query uses the full pipeline — §13.4 planning, §13.3 adaptive replica selection, §13.2 hedging, §13.10 coalescing.\n2689-\n2690-```\n2691-POST /multi-search\n2692-{\n2693-  \"queries\": [\n2694-    {\"indexUid\": \"products\", \"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 20},\n2695-    {\"indexUid\": \"products\", \"q\": \"laptop\", \"facets\": [\"brand\"], \"limit\": 0},\n2696-    {\"indexUid\": \"reviews\",  \"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 5}\n2697-  ]\n2698-}\n2699-→ 200 OK\n2700-{\"results\": [{...}, {...}, {...}]}\n2701-```\n2702-\n2703-Queries targeting the same index + replica group share HTTP/2 connections to nodes and share per-node query-plan cache lookups. Queries targeting different indexes run fully in parallel. A single slow query does not block the others; each query carries its own deadline.\n2704-\n2705-**Compatibility.** Each query in the batch translates to a standard `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` on Meilisearch CE. No node change.\n2706-\n2707-**Config.**\n2708-```yaml\n2709-multi_search:\n2710-  enabled: true\n2711-  max_queries_per_batch: 100\n2712-  total_timeout_ms: 30000\n2713-  per_query_timeout_ms: 30000\n2714-```\n2715-\n2716-**Metrics.** `miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch` (histogram), `miroir_multisearch_batches_total`, `miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total` (at least one query in a batch failed).\n2717-\n2718:**Interaction with §13.6 session pinning and §13.15 tenant affinity.** A multi-search batch can span multiple indexes, but session pinning is per-index and tenant affinity is per-request — the rules compose as follows:\n2719-\n2720:- **Session pinning (§13.6) is evaluated per sub-query.** Each sub-query in the batch independently consults the session's pending-writes map: sub-queries targeting an index with a pending write in the session respect the pin (routed to `pinned_group` for that sub-query); sub-queries for other indexes fall back to normal routing. Each sub-query independently may wait for its index's task to complete before executing.\n2721-- **Tenant affinity (§13.15) is evaluated per request.** The `X-Miroir-Tenant` header applies to the whole batch — every sub-query routes to the tenant's pinned group by default.\n2722-- **Conflict resolution: session pin wins.** If a specific sub-query's session pin selects a different group than the tenant's affinity group, the session pin takes precedence (strong consistency beats tenant isolation). The sub-query runs against the session-pinned group; a metric `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total{tenant}` records the override.\n2723-- **Explain output (§13.20) reflects the chosen routing for each sub-query separately** — the batched explain response contains one plan object per sub-query with its own `chosen_group`, `tenant_affinity_pinned`, and session-pin annotations.\n2724-\n2725----\n2726-\n2727-### 13.12 Vector and hybrid search sharding\n2728-\n2729-**Problem.** Meilisearch 1.6+ supports vector search and hybrid (BM25 + semantic) retrieval. Naïve top-K merging across shards produces wrong global rankings: a shard with few semantically-relevant documents returns low scores that compete badly against a dense shard's high scores. Sharded vector search requires a global rerank with over-fetch.\n2730-\n2731-**Mechanism.**\n2732-- **Write path.** Vectors travel with the document body and are routed identically to text — `hash(pk) % S` determines shard. Each node stores the full vector for its documents. Embedder configuration (model, dimensions, API key, semanticRatio default) is a setting and subject to the two-phase broadcast (§13.5); any divergence across nodes produces incompatible embeddings and the reconciler repairs it.\n2733-- **Read path.** Scatter with **over-fetch factor** (default 3×). For a request with `limit: L`, each shard returns up to `L × over_fetch_factor` hits with both `_semanticScore` and `_rankingScore` (Meilisearch exposes both under hybrid). The merger combines into a single global score via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) or convex combination `(1 − α) · bm25 + α · semantic`, matching Meilisearch's own hybrid formula. Global sort by combined score, apply offset/limit.\n2734-- **Pure vector.** Same path; merger uses `_semanticScore` only.\n2735-- **Pure keyword.** Same path; merger uses `_rankingScore` only.\n2736-\n2737-Over-fetching costs extra per-shard work but is the only way to produce correct global ranking for sparse semantic matches. Over-fetch factor is tunable per request via `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch`.\n2738-\n2739-**Compatibility.** Uses Meilisearch native hybrid search: `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` with `hybrid: {embedder, semanticRatio}` and `showRankingScoreDetails: true`. No node change.\n2740-\n2741-**Config.**\n2742-```yaml\n2743-vector_search:\n2744-  enabled: true\n2745-  over_fetch_factor: 3          # per-shard limit = requested limit × factor\n2746-  merge_strategy: convex        # convex | rrf\n2747-  hybrid_alpha_default: 0.5\n2748-  rrf_k: 60                     # RRF constant\n2749-```\n2750-\n--","numLines":250,"appliedLimit":250}}

Multi-target aliases are managed exclusively by §13.17 ILM; direct operator edits are refused.\n28\t580-\n29\t581-Alias flips update `current_uid` (single) or `target_uids` (multi) and `version` atomically; `history` is append-only within the retention bound.\n30\t582-\n31\t583:**4. `sessions` — read-your-writes session pins (consumed by §13.6).**\n32\t584-\n33\t585-```sql\n34\t586-CREATE TABLE sessions (\n35\t587-    session_id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t588-    last_write_mtask_id   TEXT,           -- nullable: session may exist before any write\n37\t589-    last_write_at         INTEGER,\n38\t590-    pinned_group          INTEGER,        -- group_id that first reached per-group quorum; nullable when pin cleared\n39\t591-    min_settings_version  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n40\t592-    ttl                   INTEGER NOT NULL -- expiry timestamp (ms since epoch); default 15m from last use\n41\t593-);\n42\t594-```\n43\t595-\n44\t596-LRU-bounded by `session_pinning.max_sessions`. Rows past `ttl` are evicted lazily on access.\n45\t597-\n46\t598-**5. `idempotency_cache` — write deduplication (consumed by §13.10).**\n47\t599-\n48\t600-```sql\n49\t601-CREATE TABLE idempotency_cache (\n50\t602-    key              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n51\t603-    body_sha256      BLOB NOT NULL,\n52\t604-    miroir_task_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n53\t605-    expires_at       INTEGER NOT NULL\n54\t606-);\n55\t607-```\n56\t608-\n57\t609-TTL defaults to 24h (`idempotency.ttl_seconds`). LRU-bounded by `idempotency.max_cached_keys`.\n58\t610-\n59\t611-**6. `jobs` — work-queued background jobs (consumed by §14.5 Mode C).**\n60\t612-\n61\t613-```sql\n62\t--\n63\t977-- `miroir_settings_version_stale` — no covering set could be assembled after excluding nodes whose `node_settings_version` is below the client's `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` floor (HTTP 503). See §13.5.\n64\t978-- `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` — client write targeted a multi-target alias managed by §13.17 ILM (HTTP 409). See §13.7.\n65\t979-- `miroir_jwt_invalid` — bearer token parsed as a JWT but failed validation (bad signature, expired, wrong `kid`/`alg`, or malformed claims) (HTTP 401). See §13.21 and \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n66\t980-- `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` — JWT signature, expiry, and index-binding are valid, but the token's `scope` array does not include the action for this method+path combination, or the `idx` claim does not match the target index (HTTP 403). Enforced orchestrator-side before any node call; see §13.21.\n67\t981-- `miroir_invalid_auth` — credentials did not match any expected key (master, admin, or valid search UI JWT) on an auth-gated endpoint (HTTP 401). See \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n68\t982-\n69\t983-### Reserved fields\n70\t984-\n71\t985-[Omitted long context line]\n72\t986-\n73\t987-| Field | Type | Reserved when | Purpose |\n74\t988-|-------|------|---------------|---------|\n75\t989-| `_miroir_shard` | integer | Always (unconditional) | Shard identity injected at write; stored as a filterable attribute; used by the rebalancer (Section 4) and anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) for targeted shard-level retrieval. |\n76\t990-| `_miroir_updated_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8) | Logical write timestamp used as the authoritative-version tie-breaker during anti-entropy repair. |\n77\t991-| `_miroir_expires_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `ttl.enabled: true` (§13.14) | Per-document expiration timestamp consumed by the TTL sweeper. Added to `filterableAttributes` automatically at index creation via the two-phase settings broadcast when TTL is enabled. |\n78\t992-\n79\t993-When anti-entropy is disabled, `_miroir_updated_at` is not reserved: client values in a field of that name are preserved and passed through untouched. When anti-entropy is enabled, clients must not submit `_miroir_updated_at`; the orchestrator stamps it on every write and rejects client-supplied values with `miroir_reserved_field`.\n80\t994-\n81\t995-When TTL is disabled, `_miroir_expires_at` is not reserved and client values pass through untouched. When TTL is enabled, clients set `_miroir_expires_at` on documents that should expire; the orchestrator does not stamp it, but rejects reads that attempt to project it (it is always stripped from responses) and the sweeper (§13.14) deletes documents whose `_miroir_expires_at <= now_ms`.\n82\t996-\n83\t997-### Custom HTTP headers\n84\t998-\n85\t999-Miroir adds a small set of custom headers on top of the Meilisearch REST API. All Miroir-specific headers use the `X-Miroir-` prefix; the one exception is `Idempotency-Key`, which follows the widely recognized cross-vendor convention. Clients that do not recognize these headers MUST ignore them — Meilisearch-compatibility is preserved. Unless otherwise noted, the headers are safe to strip or forward through intermediaries.\n86\t1000-\n87\t1001-| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n88\t1002-|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n89\t1003-| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n90\t1004-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n91\t1005-| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n92\t1006-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n93\t1007:| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n94\t1008-| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n95\t1009-| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n96\t1010-| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n97\t1011-| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n98\t1012-| `X-CSRF-Token` | Request | Admin UI CSRF double-submit token; required on state-changing requests from the Admin UI session cookie path. Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n99\t1013-| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n100\t1014-\n101\t1015-### Bearer token dispatch\n102\t1016-\n103\t1017-Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n104\t1018-\n105\t1019-[Omitted long context line]\n106\t1020-\n107\t1021-[Omitted long context line]\n108\t1022-2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n109\t1023-   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n110\t1024-   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n111\t1025-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n112\t1026-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n113\t1027-3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n114\t1028-4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n115\t1029-[Omitted long context line]\n116\t1030-   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n117\t1031-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n118\t1032-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n119\t1033-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode` (`public` / `shared_key` via `X-Search-UI-Key` header / `oauth_proxy` via upstream-proxy headers); see §13.21.\n120\t1034-   - `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point (static assets); IP rate-limited only.\n121\t1035-\n122\t1036-`X-Admin-Key` (admin endpoints) and `X-Search-UI-Key` (session-minting endpoint) are evaluated independently of the bearer chain above — they short-circuit directly into their respective code paths.\n123\t1037-\n124\t--\n125\t2383-\n126\t2384-**Problem.** Section 3 flags settings broadcast as \"the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle.\" The current sequential apply-with-rollback leaves a non-atomic window where some nodes have new settings and others have old, producing non-comparable `_rankingScore` values and corrupting merged search results (Open Problem 4).\n127\t2385-\n128\t2386-**Mechanism.** Replace the sequential flow with propose / verify / commit. Meilisearch has no dry-run, so verification is read-back:\n129\t2387-\n130\t2388-```\n131\t2389-Phase 1 — Propose (parallel):\n132\t2390-    for each node:\n133\t2391-        PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings   (new settings)\n134\t2392-        task_uid = await\n135\t2393-    wait for all task_uids to reach \"succeeded\"\n136\t2394-\n137\t2395-Phase 2 — Verify (parallel):\n138\t2396-    for each node:\n139\t2397-        actual      = GET /indexes/{uid}/settings\n140\t2398-        actual_hash = sha256(canonical_json(actual))\n141\t2399-    all hashes must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed))\n142\t2400-\n143\t2401-Phase 3 — Commit:\n144\t2402-    if verify ok: increment settings_version in task store;\n145\t2403-                  stamp X-Miroir-Settings-Version on future responses\n146\t2404-    if diverge:   reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair);\n147\t2405-                  after max_retries, freeze writes on that index and\n148\t2406-                  raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.\n149\t2407-```\n150\t2408-\n151\t2409-During phases 1–2 the orchestrator returns 202 with a \"pending\" status; clients poll `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}`. Reads during that window include an `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.\n152\t2410-\n153\t2411-**Drift reconciler (always on).** A background task runs every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5m), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. This catches out-of-band changes (direct access to a node) and cures drift without operator intervention.\n154\t2412-\n155\t2413:[Omitted long matching line]\n156\t2414-\n157\t2415-**Compatibility.** Uses `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings` and `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` — both public endpoints.\n158\t2416-\n159\t2417-**Config.**\n160\t2418-```yaml\n161\t2419-miroir:\n162\t2420-  settings_broadcast:\n163\t2421-    strategy: two_phase             # two_phase | sequential (legacy)\n164\t2422-    verify_timeout_s: 60\n165\t2423-    max_repair_retries: 3\n166\t2424-    freeze_writes_on_unrepairable: true\n167\t2425-  settings_drift_check:\n168\t2426-    interval_s: 300\n169\t2427-    auto_repair: true\n170\t2428-```\n171\t2429-\n172\t2430-**Metrics.** `miroir_settings_broadcast_phase` (gauge), `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total`, `miroir_settings_drift_repair_total`, `miroir_settings_version` (gauge; increments only on successful commit).\n173\t2431-\n174\t2432----\n175\t2433-\n176\t2434-### 13.6 Read-your-writes via session pinning\n177\t2435-\n178\t2436-**Problem.** Miroir is eventually consistent per the task reconciliation model. Clients reading immediately after writing race against node task processing and frequently fail. SDKs work around this by polling task status — clumsy and error-prone.\n179\t2437-\n180\t2438-**Mechanism.** Introduce a session identified by `X-Miroir-Session: <opaque-uuid>` (clients generate and persist the value). Session state in the task store:\n181\t2439-\n182\t2440-```\n183\t2441-session_id → {\n184\t2442-    last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n185\t2443-    last_write_at: Instant,\n186\t--\n187\t2688-**Mechanism.** `POST /multi-search` with `{\"queries\": [{indexUid, q, filter, ...}, ...]}`. Each query in the batch is scattered independently and in parallel; results are returned in input order with individual status codes. Each query uses the full pipeline — §13.4 planning, §13.3 adaptive replica selection, §13.2 hedging, §13.10 coalescing.\n188\t2689-\n189\t2690-```\n190\t2691-POST /multi-search\n191\t2692-{\n192\t2693-  \"queries\": [\n193\t2694-    {\"indexUid\": \"products\", \"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 20},\n194\t2695-    {\"indexUid\": \"products\", \"q\": \"laptop\", \"facets\": [\"brand\"], \"limit\": 0},\n195\t2696-    {\"indexUid\": \"reviews\",  \"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 5}\n196\t2697-  ]\n197\t2698-}\n198\t2699-→ 200 OK\n199\t2700-{\"results\": [{...}, {...}, {...}]}\n200\t2701-```\n201\t2702-\n202\t2703-Queries targeting the same index + replica group share HTTP/2 connections to nodes and share per-node query-plan cache lookups. Queries targeting different indexes run fully in parallel. A single slow query does not block the others; each query carries its own deadline.\n203\t2704-\n204\t2705-**Compatibility.** Each query in the batch translates to a standard `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` on Meilisearch CE. No node change.\n205\t2706-\n206\t2707-**Config.**\n207\t2708-```yaml\n208\t2709-multi_search:\n209\t2710-  enabled: true\n210\t2711-  max_queries_per_batch: 100\n211\t2712-  total_timeout_ms: 30000\n212\t2713-  per_query_timeout_ms: 30000\n213\t2714-```\n214\t2715-\n215\t2716-**Metrics.** `miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch` (histogram), `miroir_multisearch_batches_total`, `miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total` (at least one query in a batch failed).\n216\t2717-\n217\t2718:**Interaction with §13.6 session pinning and §13.15 tenant affinity.** A multi-search batch can span multiple indexes, but session pinning is per-index and tenant affinity is per-request — the rules compose as follows:\n218\t2719-\n219\t2720:- **Session pinning (§13.6) is evaluated per sub-query.** Each sub-query in the batch independently consults the session's pending-writes map: sub-queries targeting an index with a pending write in the session respect the pin (routed to `pinned_group` for that sub-query); sub-queries for other indexes fall back to normal routing. Each sub-query independently may wait for its index's task to complete before executing.\n220\t2721-- **Tenant affinity (§13.15) is evaluated per request.** The `X-Miroir-Tenant` header applies to the whole batch — every sub-query routes to the tenant's pinned group by default.\n221\t2722-- **Conflict resolution: session pin wins.** If a specific sub-query's session pin selects a different group than the tenant's affinity group, the session pin takes precedence (strong consistency beats tenant isolation). The sub-query runs against the session-pinned group; a metric `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total{tenant}` records the override.\n222\t2723-- **Explain output (§13.20) reflects the chosen routing for each sub-query separately** — the batched explain response contains one plan object per sub-query with its own `chosen_group`, `tenant_affinity_pinned`, and session-pin annotations.\n223\t2724-\n224\t2725----\n225\t2726-\n226\t2727-### 13.12 Vector and hybrid search sharding\n227\t2728-\n228\t2729-**Problem.** Meilisearch 1.6+ supports vector search and hybrid (BM25 + semantic) retrieval. Naïve top-K merging across shards produces wrong global rankings: a shard with few semantically-relevant documents returns low scores that compete badly against a dense shard's high scores. Sharded vector search requires a global rerank with over-fetch.\n229\t2730-\n230\t2731-**Mechanism.**\n231\t2732-- **Write path.** Vectors travel with the document body and are routed identically to text — `hash(pk) % S` determines shard. Each node stores the full vector for its documents. Embedder configuration (model, dimensions, API key, semanticRatio default) is a setting and subject to the two-phase broadcast (§13.5); any divergence across nodes produces incompatible embeddings and the reconciler repairs it.\n232\t2733-- **Read path.** Scatter with **over-fetch factor** (default 3×). For a request with `limit: L`, each shard returns up to `L × over_fetch_factor` hits with both `_semanticScore` and `_rankingScore` (Meilisearch exposes both under hybrid). The merger combines into a single global score via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) or convex combination `(1 − α) · bm25 + α · semantic`, matching Meilisearch's own hybrid formula. Global sort by combined score, apply offset/limit.\n233\t2734-- **Pure vector.** Same path; merger uses `_semanticScore` only.\n234\t2735-- **Pure keyword.** Same path; merger uses `_rankingScore` only.\n235\t2736-\n236\t2737-Over-fetching costs extra per-shard work but is the only way to produce correct global ranking for sparse semantic matches. Over-fetch factor is tunable per request via `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch`.\n237\t2738-\n238\t2739-**Compatibility.** Uses Meilisearch native hybrid search: `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` with `hybrid: {embedder, semanticRatio}` and `showRankingScoreDetails: true`. No node change.\n239\t2740-\n240\t2741-**Config.**\n241\t2742-```yaml\n242\t2743-vector_search:\n243\t2744-  enabled: true\n244\t2745-  over_fetch_factor: 3          # per-shard limit = requested limit × factor\n245\t2746-  merge_strategy: convex        # convex | rrf\n246\t2747-  hybrid_alpha_default: 0.5\n247\t2748-  rrf_k: 60                     # RRF constant\n248\t2749-```\n249\t2750-\n250\t--\n251\t\n252\t[Showing results with pagination = limit: 250]"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a289b97f-86a3-4c8c-9aaf-f321bd1872ff","uuid":"1917ed83-7626-4662-9f38-0603bbb52bb8","timestamp":"2026-05-23T04:49:11.247Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-miroir/a289b97f-86a3-4c8c-9aaf-f321bd1872ff/tool-results/call_4236dafbe4d14b1a8ffa127d.txt","content":"553-    index_uid   TEXT NOT NULL,\n554-    node_id     TEXT NOT NULL,\n555-    version     INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- last cluster-wide settings_version this (index, node) pair verified\n556-    updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n557-    PRIMARY KEY (index_uid, node_id)\n558-);\n559-```\n560-\n561-Advanced whenever a two-phase verify (§13.5 Phase 2) or drift-repair cycle succeeds for that pair. Covering-set construction for reads carrying `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` excludes rows where `version < floor`.\n562-\n563-**3. `aliases` — atomic index aliases (consumed by §13.7, extended for multi-target by §13.17).**\n564-\n565-```sql\n566-CREATE TABLE aliases (\n567-    name          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n568-    kind          TEXT NOT NULL,          -- 'single' | 'multi'\n569-    current_uid   TEXT,                   -- non-null when kind='single'\n570-    target_uids   TEXT,                   -- JSON array of UIDs; non-null when kind='multi'\n571-    version       INTEGER NOT NULL,       -- monotonic flip counter\n572-    created_at    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n573-    history       TEXT NOT NULL           -- JSON array: last N prior states, bounded by aliases.history_retention\n574-);\n575-```\n576-\n577-Two alias types are supported:\n578-- **Single-target alias (`kind='single'`)** — one `current_uid`; client writes and reads both resolve to that UID; atomic flip via `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}`.\n579-- **Multi-target alias (`kind='multi'`)** — `target_uids` is a JSON array of concrete UIDs; reads fan out across all of them via §13.11 multi-search and merge by `_rankingScore`; writes are rejected with `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` (see §13.7). Multi-target aliases are managed exclusively by §13.17 ILM; direct operator edits are refused.\n580-\n581-Alias flips update `current_uid` (single) or `target_uids` (multi) and `version` atomically; `history` is append-only within the retention bound.\n582-\n583:**4. `sessions` — read-your-writes session pins (consumed by §13.6).**\n584-\n585-```sql\n586-CREATE TABLE sessions (\n587-    session_id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n588-    last_write_mtask_id   TEXT,           -- nullable: session may exist before any write\n589-    last_write_at         INTEGER,\n590-    pinned_group          INTEGER,        -- group_id that first reached per-group quorum; nullable when pin cleared\n591-    min_settings_version  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n592-    ttl                   INTEGER NOT NULL -- expiry timestamp (ms since epoch); default 15m from last use\n593-);\n594-```\n595-\n596-LRU-bounded by `session_pinning.max_sessions`.
