LRU-bounded by `idempotency.max_cached_keys`.\n610-\n611-**6. `jobs` — work-queued background jobs (consumed by §14.5 Mode C).**\n612-\n613-```sql\n--\n977-- `miroir_settings_version_stale` — no covering set could be assembled after excluding nodes whose `node_settings_version` is below the client's `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` floor (HTTP 503). See §13.5.\n978-- `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` — client write targeted a multi-target alias managed by §13.17 ILM (HTTP 409). See §13.7.\n979-- `miroir_jwt_invalid` — bearer token parsed as a JWT but failed validation (bad signature, expired, wrong `kid`/`alg`, or malformed claims) (HTTP 401). See §13.21 and \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n980-- `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` — JWT signature, expiry, and index-binding are valid, but the token's `scope` array does not include the action for this method+path combination, or the `idx` claim does not match the target index (HTTP 403). Enforced orchestrator-side before any node call; see §13.21.\n981-- `miroir_invalid_auth` — credentials did not match any expected key (master, admin, or valid search UI JWT) on an auth-gated endpoint (HTTP 401). See \"Bearer token dispatch\" below.\n982-\n983-### Reserved fields\n984-\n985-[Omitted long context line]\n986-\n987-| Field | Type | Reserved when | Purpose |\n988-|-------|------|---------------|---------|\n989-| `_miroir_shard` | integer | Always (unconditional) | Shard identity injected at write; stored as a filterable attribute; used by the rebalancer (Section 4) and anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) for targeted shard-level retrieval. |\n990-| `_miroir_updated_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8) | Logical write timestamp used as the authoritative-version tie-breaker during anti-entropy repair. |\n991-| `_miroir_expires_at` | integer (ms since epoch) | Only when `ttl.enabled: true` (§13.14) | Per-document expiration timestamp consumed by the TTL sweeper. Added to `filterableAttributes` automatically at index creation via the two-phase settings broadcast when TTL is enabled. |\n992-\n993-When anti-entropy is disabled, `_miroir_updated_at` is not reserved: client values in a field of that name are preserved and passed through untouched. When anti-entropy is enabled, clients must not submit `_miroir_updated_at`; the orchestrator stamps it on every write and rejects client-supplied values with `miroir_reserved_field`.\n994-\n995-When TTL is disabled, `_miroir_expires_at` is not reserved and client values pass through untouched. When TTL is enabled, clients set `_miroir_expires_at` on documents that should expire; the orchestrator does not stamp it, but rejects reads that attempt to project it (it is always stripped from responses) and the sweeper (§13.14) deletes documents whose `_miroir_expires_at <= now_ms`.\n996-\n997-### Custom HTTP headers\n998-\n999-Miroir adds a small set of custom headers on top of the Meilisearch REST API. All Miroir-specific headers use the `X-Miroir-` prefix; the one exception is `Idempotency-Key`, which follows the widely recognized cross-vendor convention. Clients that do not recognize these headers MUST ignore them — Meilisearch-compatibility is preserved. Unless otherwise noted, the headers are safe to strip or forward through intermediaries.\n1000-\n1001-| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n1002-|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n1003-| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n1004-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n1005-| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n1006-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n1007:| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n1008-| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n1009-| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n1010-| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n1011-| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n1012-| `X-CSRF-Token` | Request | Admin UI CSRF double-submit token; required on state-changing requests from the Admin UI session cookie path. Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n1013-| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n1014-\n1015-### Bearer token dispatch\n1016-\n1017-Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n1018-\n1019-[Omitted long context line]\n1020-\n1021-[Omitted long context line]\n1022-2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n1023-   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n1024-   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n1025-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n1026-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n1027-3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n1028-4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n1029-[Omitted long context line]\n1030-   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n1031-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n1032-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n1033-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode` (`public` / `shared_key` via `X-Search-UI-Key` header / `oauth_proxy` via upstream-proxy headers); see §13.21.\n1034-   - `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point (static assets); IP rate-limited only.\n1035-\n1036-`X-Admin-Key` (admin endpoints) and `X-Search-UI-Key` (session-minting endpoint) are evaluated independently of the bearer chain above — they short-circuit directly into their respective code paths.\n1037-\n--\n2383-\n2384-**Problem.** Section 3 flags settings broadcast as \"the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle.\" The current sequential apply-with-rollback leaves a non-atomic window where some nodes have new settings and others have old, producing non-comparable `_rankingScore` values and corrupting merged search results (Open Problem 4).\n2385-\n2386-**Mechanism.** Replace the sequential flow with propose / verify / commit. Meilisearch has no dry-run, so verification is read-back:\n2387-\n2388-```\n2389-Phase 1 — Propose (parallel):\n2390-    for each node:\n2391-        PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings   (new settings)\n2392-        task_uid = await\n2393-    wait for all task_uids to reach \"succeeded\"\n2394-\n2395-Phase 2 — Verify (parallel):\n2396-    for each node:\n2397-        actual      = GET /indexes/{uid}/settings\n2398-        actual_hash = sha256(canonical_json(actual))\n2399-    all hashes must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed))\n2400-\n2401-Phase 3 — Commit:\n2402-    if verify ok: increment settings_version in task store;\n2403-                  stamp X-Miroir-Settings-Version on future responses\n2404-    if diverge:   reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair);\n2405-                  after max_retries, freeze writes on that index and\n2406-                  raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.\n2407-```\n2408-\n2409-During phases 1–2 the orchestrator returns 202 with a \"pending\" status; clients poll `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}`. Reads during that window include an `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.\n2410-\n2411-**Drift reconciler (always on).** A background task runs every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5m), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. This catches out-of-band changes (direct access to a node) and cures drift without operator intervention.\n2412-\n2413:[Omitted long matching line]\n2414-\n2415-**Compatibility.** Uses `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings` and `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` — both public endpoints.\n2416-\n2417-**Config.**\n2418-```yaml\n2419-miroir:\n2420-  settings_broadcast:\n2421-    strategy: two_phase             # two_phase | sequential (legacy)\n2422-    verify_timeout_s: 60\n2423-    max_repair_retries: 3\n2424-    freeze_writes_on_unrepairable: true\n2425-  settings_drift_check:\n2426-    interval_s: 300\n2427-    auto_repair: true\n2428-```\n2429-\n2430-**Metrics.** `miroir_settings_broadcast_phase` (gauge), `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total`, `miroir_settings_drift_repair_total`, `miroir_settings_version` (gauge; increments only on successful commit).\n2431-\n2432----\n2433-\n2434-### 13.6 Read-your-writes via session pinning\n2435-\n2436-**Problem.** Miroir is eventually consistent per the task reconciliation model. Queries targeting different indexes run fully in parallel. A single slow query does not block the others; each query carries its own deadline.\n2704-\n2705-**Compatibility.** Each query in the batch translates to a standard `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` on Meilisearch CE. No node change.\n2706-\n2707-**Config.**\n2708-```yaml\n2709-multi_search:\n2710-  enabled: true\n2711-  max_queries_per_batch: 100\n2712-  total_timeout_ms: 30000\n2713-  per_query_timeout_ms: 30000\n2714-```\n2715-\n2716-**Metrics.** `miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch` (histogram), `miroir_multisearch_batches_total`, `miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total` (at least one query in a batch failed).\n2717-\n2718:**Interaction with §13.6 session pinning and §13.15 tenant affinity.** A multi-search batch can span multiple indexes, but session pinning is per-index and tenant affinity is per-request — the rules compose as follows:\n2719-\n2720:- **Session pinning (§13.6) is evaluated per sub-query.** Each sub-query in the batch independently consults the session's pending-writes map: sub-queries targeting an index with a pending write in the session respect the pin (routed to `pinned_group` for that sub-query); sub-queries for other indexes fall back to normal routing. Each sub-query independently may wait for its index's task to complete before executing.\n2721-- **Tenant affinity (§13.15) is evaluated per request.** The `X-Miroir-Tenant` header applies to the whole batch — every sub-query routes to the tenant's pinned group by default.\n2722-- **Conflict resolution: session pin wins.** If a specific sub-query's session pin selects a different group than the tenant's affinity group, the session pin takes precedence (strong consistency beats tenant isolation). The sub-query runs against the session-pinned group; a metric `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total{tenant}` records the override.\n2723-- **Explain output (§13.20) reflects the chosen routing for each sub-query separately** — the batched explain response contains one plan object per sub-query with its own `chosen_group`, `tenant_affinity_pinned`, and session-pin annotations.\n2724-\n2725----\n2726-\n2727-### 13.12 Vector and hybrid search sharding\n2728-\n2729-**Problem.** Meilisearch 1.6+ supports vector search and hybrid (BM25 + semantic) retrieval. Naïve top-K merging across shards produces wrong global rankings: a shard with few semantically-relevant documents returns low scores that compete badly against a dense shard's high scores. Sharded vector search requires a global rerank with over-fetch.\n2730-\n2731-**Mechanism.**\n2732-- **Write path.** Vectors travel with the document body and are routed identically to text — `hash(pk) % S` determines shard. Each node stores the full vector for its documents. Embedder configuration (model, dimensions, API key, semanticRatio default) is a setting and subject to the two-phase broadcast (§13.5); any divergence across nodes produces incompatible embeddings and the reconciler repairs it.\n2733-- **Read path.** Scatter with **over-fetch factor** (default 3×). For a request with `limit: L`, each shard returns up to `L × over_fetch_factor` hits with both `_semanticScore` and `_rankingScore` (Meilisearch exposes both under hybrid). The merger combines into a single global score via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) or convex combination `(1 − α) · bm25 + α · semantic`, matching Meilisearch's own hybrid formula. Global sort by combined score, apply offset/limit.\n2734-- **Pure vector.** Same path; merger uses `_semanticScore` only.\n2735-- **Pure keyword.** Same path; merger uses `_rankingScore` only.\n2736-\n2737-Over-fetching costs extra per-shard work but is the only way to produce correct global ranking for sparse semantic matches. Over-fetch factor is tunable per request via `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch`.\n2738-\n2739-**Compatibility.** Uses Meilisearch native hybrid search: `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` with `hybrid: {embedder, semanticRatio}` and `showRankingScoreDetails: true`. No node change.\n2740-\n2741-**Config.**\n2742-```yaml\n2743-vector_search:\n2744-  enabled: true\n2745-  over_fetch_factor: 3          # per-shard limit = requested limit × factor\n2746-  merge_strategy: convex        # convex | rrf\n2747-  hybrid_alpha_default: 0.5\n2748-  rrf_k: 60                     # RRF constant\n2749-```\n2750-\n--\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 250]","numLines":252,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}

write\n//! - Block strategy: read blocks until write completes\n//! - RoutePin strategy: read routes to pinned group without waiting\n//! - Session TTL and LRU eviction\n//! - Pinned group failure .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session is recorded\n    let session = mtask_id.clone(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify pending write exists\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n\n    // Clear pending write (simulating task completion)\n    manager.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n\n    // Verify no pending write\n manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_expiration() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1, // 1 second TTL for testing\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-expire\";\n    let mtask_id = immediately\n    assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Wait for expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // Session should be expired\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Pinned group should be None for expired session\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_max_sessions_lru_eviction() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        max_sessions: 2,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add first session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add second session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add third session (should evict first session)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // First session should be evicted\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Second and third should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-fail\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-fail\".to_string();\n    let pinned_group = 1;\n\n    // Record a write\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify pin is set\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(pinned_group)\n    );\n\n    // Simulate pinned group failure\n    let cleared = manager\n        .handle_pinned_group_failure(session_id, pinned_group)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(cleared);\n\n    // Pin should be cleared\n    assert_eq!(manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_wait_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::RoutePin);\n\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"block\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n\n    // Unknown strategy defaults to Block\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"unknown\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_prune_expired_sessions() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add sessions\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-expire\", \"mtask-b\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Wait for expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // Refresh session-active to keep it alive\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a-refreshed\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add another active session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active-2\", \"mtask-c\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Prune expired\n    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n\n    // Should have pruned 1 expired session (session-expire)\n    assert_eq!(pruned, 1);\n\n    // Expired session should be gone\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-expire\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Active sessions should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active-2\").await.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_delete_session() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-delete\";\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-delete\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session exists\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.is_some());\n\n    // Delete session\n    let deleted = manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(deleted);\n\n    // Verify session is gone\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.is_none());\n\n    // Delete again should return false\n    let deleted_again = manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(!deleted_again);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_count() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 0);\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 1);\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 2);\n\n    manager.delete_session(\"session-1\").await;\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 1);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_multiple_writes_same_session_preserves_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-multi\";\n    let first_group = 2;\n\n    // First write pins to group 2\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-1\".to_string(), first_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n        Some(first_group)\n    );\n\n    // Second write tries to pin to group 3 (should be ignored)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 3)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Pin should still be group 2 (first write wins)\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n        Some(first_group)\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_max_wait_duration() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        max_wait_ms: 10000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.max_wait_duration().as_millis(), 10000);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_enabled_flag() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        enabled: false,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert!(!manager.is_enabled());\n\n    // When disabled, record_write should succeed but be no-op\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n74\t        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n75\t            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n76\t        }\n77\t        result\n78\t    }\n79\t}\n80\t\n81\tfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n82\t    match e {\n83\t \"timeout\",\n86\t    }\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\tpub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\n90\twhere\n91\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n92\t{\n93\t    axum::Router::new()\n94\t        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n95\t}\n96\t\n97\t/// Search request body.\n98\t#[derive(Deserialize)]\n99\tstruct SearchRequestBody {\n100\t    q: Option<String>,\n101\t    offset: Option<usize>,\n102\t    limit: Option<usize>,\n103\t    filter: Option<Value>,\n104\t    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n105\t    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n106\t    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n107\t    #[serde(flatten)]\n108\t    rest: Value,\n109\t}\n110\t\n111\timpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n112\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n113\t        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n114\t            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n115\t            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n116\t            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n117\t            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n118\t            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n119\t            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n120\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n121\t    }\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n125\t///\n126\t/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n127\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n128\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n129\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n130\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n131\t///\n132\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n133\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n134\t/// explicitly requested it.\n135\t///\n136\t/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n137\t/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n138\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n139\tasync fn search_handler(\n140\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n141\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n142\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n143\t    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n144\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n145\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n146\t    let start = Instant::now();\n147\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n148\t\n149\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n150\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n151\t        let s = ext.0;\n152\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n153\t    });\n154\t\n155\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n156\t    let source_ip = headers\n157\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n158\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n159\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n160\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n161\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n162\t        .trim()\n163\t        .to_string();\n164\t\n165\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n166\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n167\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n168\t        Err(e) => {\n169\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n170\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n171\t        }\n172\t    };\n173\t\n174\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n175\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n176\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n177\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n178\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n179\t                    if !allowed {\n180\t                        warn!(\n181\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n182\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n183\t                        );\n184\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n185\t                    }\n186\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n187\t                }\n188\t                Err(e) => {\n189\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n190\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n191\t                }\n192\t            }\n193\t        }\n194\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n195\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n196\t            &source_ip,\n197\t            limit,\n198\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n199\t        );\n200\t        if !allowed {\n201\t            warn!(\n202\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n203\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n204\t            );\n205\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n206\t        }\n207\t    }\n208\t\n209\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n210\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n211\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n212\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n213\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n214\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n215\t            // It's an alias\n216\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n217\t        } else {\n218\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n219\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n220\t        }\n221\t    } else {\n222\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n223\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n224\t    };\n225\t\n226\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n227\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n228\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n229\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n230\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n231\t            match strategy {\n232\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n233\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n234\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n235\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n236\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n237\t                        sid,\n238\t                        &state.task_registry,\n239\t                        max_wait,\n240\t                    ).await {\n241\t                        Ok(true) => {\n242\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n243\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n244\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n245\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n246\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n247\t                        }\n248\t                        Ok(false) => {\n249\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n250\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n251\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n252\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n253\t                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n254\t                        }\n255\t                        Err(_) => {\n256\t                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n257\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n258\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n259\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n260\t                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n261\t                        }\n262\t                    }\n263\t                }\n264\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n265\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n266\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n267\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n268\t                }\n269\t            }\n270\t        } else {\n271\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n272\t            (None, None)\n273\t        }\n274\t    } else {\n275\t        // No session header - normal routing\n276\t        (None, None)\n277\t    };\n278\t\n279\t    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n280\t    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n281\t        debug!(\n282\t            session_id = ?sid,\n283\t            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n284\t            strategy = label,\n285\t            \"session pinning applied\"\n286\t        );\n287\t    }\n288\t\n289\t    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n290\t    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n291\t    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n292\t    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n293\t        return search_multi_targets(\n294\t            resolved_targets,\n295\t            body,\n296\t            state,\n297\t            headers,\n298\t            sid,\n299\t            client_requested_score,\n300\t        ).await;\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n304\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n305\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n306\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n307\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n308\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n309\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n310\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n311\t                &state.pod_id,\n312\t                &index,\n313\t                sk.generation,\n314\t            );\n315\t\n316\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n317\t            sk.primary_key\n318\t        } else {\n319\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n320\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n321\t        }\n322\t    } else {\n323\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n324\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n325\t    };\n326\t\n327\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n328\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n329\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n330\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n331\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n332\t\n333\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n334\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n335\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n336\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n337\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n338\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n339\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n340\t    };\n341\t\n342\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n343\t    let plan = {\n344\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n345\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n346\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n347\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n348\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n349\t            min_settings_version,\n350\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n351\t        ).entered();\n352\t\n353\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n354\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n355\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n356\t                &topo,\n357\t                0,\n358\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n359\t                state.config.shards,\n360\t                group,\n361\t            ) {\n362\t                Some(p) => p,\n363\t                None => {\n364\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n365\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n366\t                    warn!(\n367\t                        pinned_group = group,\n368\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n369\t                    );\n370\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n371\t                }\n372\t            }\n373\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n374\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n375\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n376\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n377\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n378\t                &topo,\n379\t                0,\n380\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n381\t                state.config.shards,\n382\t                &index,\n383\t                floor,\n384\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n385\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n386\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n387\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n388\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n389\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n390\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n391\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n392\t                        })\n393\t                    })\n394\t                },\n395\t            );\n396\t\n397\t            match plan_result {\n398\t                Some(p) => p,\n399\t                None => {\n400\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n401\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n402\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n403\t                        format!(\n404\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n405\t                            floor, index\n406\t                        ),\n407\t                    );\n408\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n409\t                }\n410\t            }\n411\t        } else {\n412\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n413\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n414\t        }\n415\t    };\n416\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n417\t\n418\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n419\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n420\t\n421\t    // Build search request\n422\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n423\t        index_uid: facets: body.facets,\n429\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n430\t        body: body.rest,\n431\t        global_idf: None,\n432\t    };\n433\t\n434\t    // Create node client with the ));\n439\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n440\t\n441\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n442\t    let strategy = failed\");\n456\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n457\t    })?;\n458\t\n459\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n460\t    drop(topo);\n461\t\n462\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n463\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n464\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    // Build response body\n468\t    let mut body = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n477\t    }\n478\t\n479\t    // Build response with optional headers\n480\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n481\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n482\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n483\t\n484\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n485\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n486\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n490\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n491\t    if current_version > 0 {\n492\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n493\t    }\n494\t\n495\t    if result.degraded {\n496\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n500\t        let mut sorted_shards = .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n505\t            .join(\",\");\n506\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n507\t    } else if result.degraded {\n508\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n509\t    }\n510\t\n511\t    let response = response\n512\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n513\t        .unwrap();\n514\t\n515\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n516\t    // request_id and pod_id are included %effective_index,\n521\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n522\t        node_count = node_count,\n523\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n524\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n525\t        \"search completed\"\n526\t    );\n527\t\n528\t    Ok(response)\n529\t}\n530\t\n531\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n532\t///\n533\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n534\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n535\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n536\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n537\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n538\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n539\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n540\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n541\t                return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n578\t                    }\n579\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n580\t                }\n581\t                Err(e) => {\n582\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n583\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n584\t                }\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n588\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n589\t            &source_ip,\n590\t            limit,\n591\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n592\t        );\n593\t        if !allowed {\n594\t            warn!(\n595\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n596\t                \"search UI rate        // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n606\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n607\t            match strategy {\n608\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n609\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n610\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n611\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n612\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n613\t                        sid,\n614\t                        &state.task_registry,\n615\t                        max_wait,\n616\t                    ).await {\n617\t                        Ok(true) => {\n618\t                            // Write succeeded, state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n621\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n622\t                            (None::<u32>, state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n629\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n630\t                        }\n631\t                        Err(_) => {\n632\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n633\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n634\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n635\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n636\t                            (Some(group), &state.pod_id,\n668\t                primary_target,\n669\t                sk.generation,\n670\t            );\n671\t            sk.primary_key\n672\t        } else {\n673\t \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n691\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n692\t    };\n693\t\n694\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read       );\n722\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n723\t                }\n724\t            }\n725\t        } else if let
