Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n    //\n    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n\n    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\", \"_miroir_metadata\": {\"key\": \"val\"}});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_custom\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_metadata\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n    ///\n    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n        struct TestCase {\n            doc: Value,\n            description: &'static str,\n            has_shard: bool,\n            has_updated_at: bool,\n            has_expires_at: bool,\n        }\n\n        let test_cases = vec![\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        for tc in test_cases {\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n // 1. Client sends document WITHOUT _miroir_shard\n        // 2. Validation passes (no _miroir_shard present)\n        // 3. Orchestrator injects _miroir_shard for routing\n        // 4. Write proceeds with injected field\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n\n        // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation at line 279-290)\n        let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(5);\n\n        assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], 5, \"orchestrator should inject _miroir_shard\");\n        assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be present\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_validation_order() {\n        // _miroir_shard is checked BEFORE primary key validation (per acceptance criteria)\n        // This ensures clients can't bypass validation by including both fields\n\n        let doc_with_shard_no_pk = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n        // The validation should catch _miroir_shard first, not missing primary key\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (TTL) |\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard: Always reserved regardless of config\n Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct SessionId(pub String);\n\nimpl Default for SessionId {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self(String::new())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionId {\n    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n    ///\n    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\npub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    requests_total: CounterVec,\n    requests_in_flight: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Node health metrics ──\n    node_healthy: GaugeVec,\n    node_request_duration: HistogramVec,\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\\n}\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn test_session_expiration() {\\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\\n Session should be expired\\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\\n\\n    // Pinned group should be None for expired session\\n    let pinned_group = test_multiple_writes_same_session_preserves_pin() {\\n    let manager = default_manager();\\n\\n    let session_id = \\\"test-session-multi\\\";\\n    let first_group = 2;\\n\\n    // First write pins to group 2\\n    manager\\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \\\"mtask-1\\\".to_string(), first_group)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap();\\n\\n    assert_eq!(\\n mtask_id.clone(),\\n            Task {\\n                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\\n                node_task_ids: HashMap::new(),\\n                status,\\n                index_uid: None,\\n    .as_millis() as u64,\\n            },\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Update a task's status.\\n    async fn update_task(&self, mtask_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(mtask_id) {\\n            task.status = status;\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\\nimpl TaskRegistry for Err(\\\"unimplemented\\\".into())\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Acceptance test 1: Write + session + immediate read with `block` → read sees the write.\\n///\\n/// This test verifies that when using the \\\"block\\\" wait strategy, a read with a session\\n/// header will wait for the pending write to complete before proceeding.\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_block_strategy() {\\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\\n        wait_strategy: \\\"block\\\".to_string(),\\n        max_wait_ms: 5000,\\n        ..Default::default()\\n    };\\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\\n    let task_registry = Arc::new(MockTaskRegistry::new());\\n\\n    let session_id = \\\"test-session-block-read\\\";\\n acceptance_session_ttl_and_lru_eviction() {\\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\\n        ttl_seconds: 1,\\n        max_sessions: 3,\\n        ..Default::default()\\n    };\\n    let manager = .record_write_with_quorum(\\\"session-4\\\", \\\"mtask-4\\\".to_string(), 0)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap();\\n\\n    // Session-1 should be evicted\\n Session pinning acceptance tests (plan §13.6).\n//!\n//! // Record a write with session header\n    let session_id = \"test-session-1\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-123\".to_string();\n    let first_quorum_group = 2;\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session is recorded\n    let session = assert!(session.has_pending_write());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_read_with_pending_write_returns_pinned_group() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // Record a write\n    let ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nuse miroir_core::task::{Task, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Mock task registry for testing session pinning wait behavior.\nstruct MockTaskRegistry {\n    tasks: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, Task>>>,\n}\n\nimpl MockTaskRegistry {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            tasks: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a task with a specific status.\n    async fn add_task(&self, mtask_id: String, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        tasks.insert(\n            mtask_id.clone(),\n            Task {\n                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\n                node_task_ids: HashMap::new(),\n                status,\n                index_uid: None,\n                task_type: None,\n                created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .unwrap()\n                    .as_millis() as u64,\n            },\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Update a task's status.\n    async fn update_task(&self, mtask_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(mtask_id) {\n            task.status = status;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl TaskRegistry for MockTaskRegistry {\n    async fn get(&self, mtask_id: &str) -> std::result::Result<Option<Task>, Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        Ok(tasks.get(mtask_id).cloned())\n    }\n\n    async fn register(\n        &self,\n        _node_task_ids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Task, Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {\n        // For testing, return a dummy task\n        Ok(Task {\n            miroir_id: \"mtask-test\".to_string(),\n            node_task_ids: HashMap::new(),\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid: None,\n u64,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        _node_task_ids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        _index_uid: Option<String>,\n        _task_type: \"mtask-test\".to_string(),\n            node_task_ids: HashMap::new(),\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid: None,\n            task_type: None,\n            created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap()\n                .as_millis() as u64,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn list(&self, _limit: usize, _offset: usize) -> std::result::Result<Vec<Task>, Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {\n        Ok(Vec::new())\n    }\n\n    async fn cancel(&self, _mtask_id: &str) -> std::result::Result<Task, Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {\n        Err(\"unimplemented\".into())\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, _mtask_id: &str) -> std::result::Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> {\n        Err(\"unimplemented\".into())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 1: Write + session + immediate read with `block` → read sees the write.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when using the \"block\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n/// header will wait for the pending write to complete before proceeding.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_block_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"block\".to_string(),\n        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n    let task_registry = Arc::new(MockTaskRegistry::new());\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-block-read\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-block-123\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write with session pinning\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(0)\n    );\n\n    // Add the task as enqueued to the mock registry\n    task_registry.add_task(mtask_id.clone(), TaskStatus::Enqueued).await;\n\n    // Spawn a background task to complete the write after a short delay\n    let registry_clone = task_registry.clone();\n    let mtask_id_clone = mtask_id.clone();\n    tokio::spawn(async move {\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n        registry_clone.update_task(&mtask_id_clone, TaskStatus::Succeeded).await;\n    });\n\n    // Read with session should block and wait for the write to complete\n    let max_wait = Duration::from_millis(5000);\n    let result = manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n        session_id,\n        &task_registry,\n        max_wait,\n    ).await;\n\n    // The wait should succeed (write completed)\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), true);\n\n    // After completion, the session should no longer have a pending write\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 2: Write + session + immediate read with `route_pin` → read routed to pinned group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when using the \"route_pin\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n/// header routes to the pinned group without waiting for the write to complete.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_route_pin_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n    let task_registry = Arc::new(MockTaskRegistry::new());\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-route-pin\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-route-pin-123\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write with session pinning\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 1)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned to group 1\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(1)\n    );\n\n    // With route_pin strategy, we don't wait - just check the pinned group\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, Some(1));\n\n    // The pending write should still be present (not cleared by just reading)\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 3: Pinned group fails mid-session → pin cleared; read succeeds via another group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when the pinned group fails, the session pin is cleared\n/// and subsequent reads can succeed via other groups.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-group-fail\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-group-fail\".to_string();\n    let pinned_group = 2;\n\n    // Record a write that pins to group 2\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(pinned_group)\n    );\n\n    // Simulate group 2 failing\n    let cleared = manager\n        .handle_pinned_group_failure(session_id, pinned_group)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(cleared, \"Pin should be cleared when pinned group fails\");\n\n    // Pin should be cleared\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        None,\n        \"After group failure, no pinned group should be returned\"\n    );\n\n    // The session should still exist, but with no pin\n    let session = manager.get_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(session.is_some(), \"Session should still exist\");\n    assert_eq!(session.unwrap().pinned_group, None, \"Session should have no pinned group\");\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 4: Session TTL expiry and LRU eviction.\n///\n/// This test verifies that:\n/// - Sessions expire after their TTL\n/// - LRU eviction happens when max_sessions is reached\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_session_ttl_and_lru_eviction() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1,\n        max_sessions: 3,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Create sessions up to the limit\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // All three should exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n\n    // Adding a 4th session should evict the oldest (session-1)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-4\", \"mtask-4\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Session-1 should be evicted\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Sessions 2, 3, 4 should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.is_some());\n\n    // Wait for TTL expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // All remaining sessions should be expired\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Prune should remove all expired sessions\n    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n    assert_eq!(pruned, 3);\n\n    // No sessions should remain\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_none());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_none());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.is_none());\n}\n\n/// Integration test: Session pinning with scatter plan for pinned group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that `plan_search_scatter_for_group` correctly routes\n/// all shards to the pinned replica group.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_session_pin_with_scatter_plan() {\n    use miroir_core::scatter::plan_search_scatter_for_group;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\n\n    // Build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology\n    let mut topo = format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-group0-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Add nodes to group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n 1\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter_for_group(&topo, 0, 2, 4, 1);\n\n    assert!(plan.is_some(), \"Plan should be created for pinned group\");\n    let plan = plan.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the plan targets the pinned group\n    assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 1, \"Chosen group should be the pinned group\");\n\n    // Verify all shards are targeted\n    assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 4, \"All shards should be targeted\");\n\n    // Verify all selected nodes are from group 1\n    for (_shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let node = topo.node(node_id).unwrap();\n Session pinning acceptance tests (plan §13.6).\n//!\n//! Tests read-your-writes consistency via session pinning:\n//! - Write with session header → session pinned to first-quorum group\n//! - Read with session header → routes to pinned group if pending write\n//! - Block strategy: read blocks until write completes\n//! - RoutePin strategy: read routes to pinned group without waiting\n//! - Session TTL and LRU eviction\n//! - Pinned group failure default_manager() -> SessionManager {\n    test_manager(SessionPinningConfig::default())\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_write_records_session_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // Record a write with session header\n    let session_id = \"test-session-1\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-123\".to_string();\n    let first_quorum_group = 2;\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session is recorded\n    let session = manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(session.last_write_mtask_id, Some(mtask_id));\n    assert_eq!(session.pinned_group, Some(first_quorum_group));\n    assert!(session.has_pending_write());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_read_with_pending_write_returns_pinned_group() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // Record a write\n    let session_id = \"test-session-2\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-456\".to_string();\n    let first_quorum_group = 1;\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Get pinned group for read\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, Some(first_quorum_group));\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_read_without_session_returns_none() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // No session header\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(\"nonexistent\").await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_write_without_clearing_pending_write() {\n    let manager = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_expiration() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1, // 1 second TTL for testing\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-expire\";\n    let mtask_id = immediately\n    assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Wait for expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // Session should be max_sessions: 2,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add first session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add third session (should evict first session)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // First session should be evicted\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Second and third should still exist\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-fail\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-fail\".to_string();\n    let pinned_group = 1;\n\n    // Record a write\n    manager\n pinned_group)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(cleared);\n\n    // Pin should be cleared\n    assert_eq!(manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_wait_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add sessions\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n       .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add another active session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active-2\", \"mtask-c\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Prune expired\n    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n\n    // Should have pruned 1 expired session (session-expire)\n    assert_eq!(pruned, 1);\n\n    // Expired session should be gone\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-expire\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Active sessions should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active-2\").await.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_delete_session() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-delete\";\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-delete\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session exists\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.is_some());\n\n    // Delete session\n    let deleted = manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(deleted);\n\n    // Verify session is gone\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.is_none());\n\n    // Delete again should return false\n    let deleted_again = manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(!deleted_again);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_count() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n
